scope: Detected stack: Next.js 14, React 18, TypeScript/TSX/JSX, Node.js, Python tooling, SQL.js, PostgreSQL/Aurora DSQL, DynamoDB, AWS SDK, Vercel.
Static source inventory: 39 files; 33 app modules under app/, components/, and lib/; 24 connected-alive modules.

Connected scope: app/layout.tsx, app/page.tsx, components/GlobalNav.jsx, components/LandingPage.jsx, components/EvolveScreen.jsx, components/MarketplaceScreen.jsx, components/LineageScreen.jsx, lib/run-history.js, lib/ebs.js, lib/evolution-client.ts, lib/fitness.ts, lib/mutator.ts, lib/llm.ts, lib/rate-limit.ts, lib/dsql.ts, app/api/evolve/route.ts, app/api/generate-tests/route.ts, app/api/claude/route.ts, app/api/save-run/route.ts, app/api/seal-bundle/route.ts, app/api/sandbox/python/route.ts, app/api/sandbox/javascript/route.ts, app/api/sandbox/sql/route.ts.

Static but not connected-alive in the primary graph: app/api/billing/checkout/route.ts, app/api/billing/webhook/route.ts, app/api/bounties/route.ts, app/api/ci/route.ts, app/api/keys/route.ts, app/api/usage/route.ts, lib/auth.ts, lib/dynamo.ts, lib/escrow.ts.
Auxiliary static files excluded from the 33-module application count: tests/llm_test.ts, tests/sandbox_test.ts, tools/adaptive_gene_pool.py, tools/evolution_bundle.py, tools/verify_bundle.py.

Excluded: .git, .gitignore, node_modules, vendor, dependencies, site-packages, virtual environments, caches, .next, .turbo, dist, build, target, binaries, generated/minified files, large artifacts, agent outputs; manifests used only as stack evidence.
No target files were modified and no target-repository code was executed.

observations: Preflight evidence reports 33 modules and 24 connected-alive modules.
Static inventory independently resolves 39 source files as 33 application modules plus 5 auxiliary test/tool files and 1 type module.
The 24 connected modules form the browser, evolution, LLM, sandbox, persistence, sealing, and run-history paths.
The remaining 9 application modules are statically present but outside the connected-alive primary graph.
Static patterns alone do not establish a security vulnerability.

hypotheses.statement: The connected runtime scope is exactly 24 modules within a 33-module application surface; the nine remaining application modules are statically present but not connected-alive in the primary graph.

deductions: If H1 is correct, graph traversal from app/page.tsx and its dynamic components plus the primary evolution endpoints should enumerate exactly 24 modules and exclude the nine listed modules.
If H1 is correct, the 39-file source inventory should reconcile as 33 application modules, 5 auxiliary test/tool files, and 1 type module.
evidence: find inventory counted 17 app files, 5 component files, 11 lib files, 1 types file, 2 test files, and 3 tool files.
Dynamic imports and fetch references connect the UI to evolution, LLM, sandbox, sealing, save-run, and supporting libraries.
package.json and tsconfig.json establish the detected JavaScript/TypeScript/Next.js stack and exclude tools from TypeScript compilation.
git status was clean after read-only inspection.
decision: Scope audit is limited to the 24 connected-alive modules for primary runtime triage.
Retain the nine statically present but disconnected modules as explicitly excluded secondary scope, not as absent files.
Treat 39 as static source-file count, 33 as application-module count, and 24 as connected-alive count.
No vulnerability is confirmed from scope triage.

adi.statement: The supplied 33-module/24-connected-alive preflight is best explained by a 33-module Next.js application surface with a 24-module primary runtime graph and nine statically present disconnected modules.
evidence: The repository layout contains 17 app files, 5 components, and 11 libraries.
The UI and endpoint references identify the connected runtime path.
The remaining route and library files have no primary-graph reference in the inspected static relationships.

adi.statement: A complete authorized module-graph check should reproduce 24 connected modules and reconcile all 39 source files into the stated categories.
evidence: The static inventory provides the expected reconciliation counts.
The connected list is derived from framework entrypoints, dynamic imports, and endpoint references.

adi.statement: Induction is undetermined: no runtime execution or additional connectedness experiment was performed because the requested scope triage prohibited executing arbitrary target-repository code.
evidence: Only read-only inventory, manifest inspection, import/reference inspection, and git status were performed.
Preflight counts were accepted as supplied evidence rather than revalidated by executing target code.

evidence: ADI record contains one hypothesis and three inference stages.
